response.justification: The candidate is disqualified by absence of native-level Japanese language proficiency, which is an explicit required qualification. Additionally, while the resume shows 30+ years of strong software engineering experience, the past 15 years have focused on web and full-stack development rather than embedded systems, kernel work, or security domains. The resume lacks evidence of the core technical competencies required: encryption/authentication systems, network switch operation, Linux kernel investigation, and layer-2/3 networking protocols. Most recent relevant embedded systems work dates to 2002-2011.
strengths: 30+ years of demonstrated software engineering expertise across multiple languages and platforms with staff/senior-level experience
Prior embedded systems and C language experience (National Instruments 2000-2001, Altom 2002-2004) shows some foundational capability
Strong DevOps and infrastructure competency (CI/CD, Docker, Linux administration) that could support embedded product development environments
concerns: No native-level Japanese language proficiency documented—fails explicit required qualification and appears non-negotiable for role

Career trajectory diverged from embedded/systems work 15+ years ago; current expertise concentrated in web development, full-stack engineering, and AI infrastructure rather than security or kernel domains
No evidence of security domain knowledge (encryption, authentication, tamper detection, kernel vulnerabilities) or networking expertise (switch operation, L2/L3 protocols, SNMP, NETCONF, MACsec)

key_factors.factor: 15-year career focus away from embedded/security/networking domains
Broad software engineering depth and infrastructure competency
